what_worked: community-first Reddit replies with no product mention required to be worth reading
owned content assets covering core pain frames: unattended runs, reviewable output, when it works, trial CTA
market signal research: 6-8 strong shortlist candidates per scan
proof assets shipped to public repo: START_HERE.md, docs/free-open-source-proof.md, example docs
REDDIT_LEARNINGS.md update cadence capturing real pattern observations
what_did_not_work: repetitive opening line detected across multiple posts creating canned feel
GitHub 0 stars despite full day of awareness activity
GitHub account gap: no HN submission, no Dev.to OAuth possible
browserless Reddit automation unreliable, manual Chromium fallback required
monitoring frequency vs value: 5+ passes produced same shortlist after pass 2
what_is_repetitive: same pain themes in research surfacing repeatedly (worktrees, approval loops, reviewability) — useful signal but reaction keeps generating awareness content instead of conversion content
Reddit scan output stabilizes quickly; shortlist essentially fixed after pass 2
owned content drafts keep addressing same pain frames without clear next step for convinced reader

specific_failures: GitHub account gap: HN and Dev.to inaccessible — highest-signal developer distribution channels blocked
after-star path unclear: START_HERE.md not prominent enough as visible entry point
owned content not systematically linked from Reddit comments or repo to most action-forcing piece
Reddit monitoring approaching local maximum — not the binding constraint on adoption right now

next_moves.description: Create a GitHub account for RalphWorkflow marketing activity. Without it: cannot submit to HN, cannot OAuth into Dev.to, cannot interact with GitHub issues/PRs. This is the binding constraint on high-signal developer distribution.

next_moves.action: make conversion path unmistakable in repo
next_moves.description: Add prominent 'First time? Start here' callout at top of root README linking to START_HERE.md. Ensure 'Get it running' section is clearest path forward. Make write.as trial CTA article the explicit next read after quick-start.

next_moves.action: reduce Reddit scan frequency to 2x per day
next_moves.description: One morning and one evening scan sufficient. Shortlist stabilizes after pass 2. Reclaim cycle for conversion work.

next_moves.action: do not produce more awareness content until distribution is fixed
next_moves.description: 6 owned articles exist. Problem is not content volume — it is that content is not reaching right people through right channels. Focus on distributing what exists.

what_to_keep_doing: community-first Reddit replies with no/low product mention
market signal tracking — pain themes (overnight drift, merge safety, approval loops, Claude+Codex handoffs) are consistent and reliable
proof asset shipping to public repo
working_question_answer: Fix the GitHub account gap. Reddit awareness is healthy. Owned content exists. The binding constraint is that the two highest-signal developer distribution channels (HN, Dev.to) are inaccessible because there is no GitHub account for marketing activity. Fix that, then sharpen the conversion surface at the repo so arriving traffic has a clear first-trial path.
